findings.message: New `redis` scrape job targets `redis-exporter:9121`, but no `redis-exporter` service is defined in any Compose file. `observability.yml` defines exporters explicitly (e.g. `postgres-exporter`) and there is no Redis exporter anywhere — the only Redis reference is `APP_REDIS_URL` (the data store) in services.yml/README. The scrape target is unreachable, so Prometheus will perpetually report this target as down.
findings.suggestion: Add a `redis-exporter` service (e.g. `oliver006/redis_exporter`) to `infra/compose/observability.yml`, pointed at the Redis data store, before scraping `redis-exporter:9121`; or drop this scrape job.

findings.message: New `redis` scrape job has no matching alert rule. Every other scrape surface ships an alert file (postgres_alerts.yml, otel_collector_alerts.yml, prometheus_alerts.yml, slo_alerts.yml, alertmanager_alerts.yml), but there is no redis_alerts.yml. Redis metrics will be collected with nothing watching them — failures (down exporter, memory pressure, evictions) will go unalerted.
findings.suggestion: Add `infra/observability/prometheus/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml` with at least a RedisDown / RedisExporterDown alert (mirroring postgres_alerts.yml) to match the new scrape job.

findings.message: New `redis` scrape job has no corresponding Grafana dashboard. Every other scrape surface has a dashboard (postgres.json, otel-collector.json, prometheus.json, slo.json) but there is no redis.json in infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/. The new surface is scraped but not visualized.
findings.suggestion: Add a `redis.json` dashboard under infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/ (it will be picked up by the existing provisioning provider) covering the Redis exporter metrics.
